provenance.title: Deep Learning in Asset Pricing
affiliations: Institute for Computational and Mathematical Engineering, Stanford University
Department of Management Science & Engineering, Stanford University
provenance.abstract: We propose a novel approach to estimate asset pricing models for individual stock returns that takes advantage of the vast amount of conditioning information, while keeping a fully flexible form and accounting for time-variation. Our general non-linear asset pricing model is estimated with deep neural networks applied to all U.S. equity data combined with a substantial set of macroeconomic and firm-specific information.
key_claims: GAN-based no-arbitrage model achieves out-of-sample annual Sharpe Ratio of 2.6, vs 1.7 for linear model and 0.8 for Fama-French 5
Including the no-arbitrage constraint in the learning algorithm significantly improves the risk premium signal
LSTM summarizes 178 macroeconomic time series into 4 hidden state processes capturing business cycle dynamics
GAN achieves cross-sectional R2 > 90% on all 46 anomaly-sorted decile portfolios
Non-linearities matter for interactions between characteristics, not for individual characteristics in isolation

architecture.description: Three-network GAN architecture: SDF Network (FFN+LSTM), Conditional Network (FFN+LSTM), trained via minimax adversarial game

modules.name: StateMacroRNN
modules.role: Encode macroeconomic time series history into hidden state h_t for SDF network
modules.notes: 4 hidden states (SMV=4 from hyperparameter table). Processes all 178 macro variables sequentially.

modules.name: ConditionalMacroRNN
modules.role: Encode macroeconomic time series history into hidden state h_t^g for Conditional (adversarial) network
modules.notes: 32 hidden states (CSMV=32 from hyperparameter table). Separate LSTM from SDF network.

modules.name: SDFNetwork
modules.role: Compute SDF portfolio weights omega_{t,i} from macro hidden states + firm characteristics
modules.notes: HL=2, HU=64, DR=0.95 from hyperparameter table. Output omega_{t,i} for each stock i.

modules.name: ConditionalNetwork
modules.role: Compute conditioning instruments g_{t,j} to find hardest-to-explain moment conditions
modules.notes: CHL=0 (no hidden layer), CHU=8 moments. Essentially a linear transformation of inputs.

modules.name: SDFConstructor
modules.role: Build SDF M_{t+1} = 1 - sum_i omega_{t,i} * R^e_{t+1,i}

modules.name: MomentConditionLoss
modules.role: Compute GAN pricing error loss: E[M_{t+1} * R^e_{t+1,j} * g(I_t, I_{t,j})]^2
modules.notes: Eq. (3) in paper. Weighted by sqrt(T_i/T) for unbalanced panel.

modules.name: LoadingNetwork
modules.role: Estimate beta_{t,i} = E_t[R^e_{t+1,i} * F_{t+1}] via second-moment regression
modules.notes: Same architecture as SDFNetwork. Trained separately to predict R^e * F_{t+1}.

ambiguities.description: LSTM cell size (number of units per gate) not explicitly stated — only number of hidden states output
ambiguities.primary_assumption: hidden_size == num_hidden_states (4 for SDF, 32 for conditional)

ambiguities.description: Exact normalization applied to g(.) outputs not fully specified

equations.name: GAN Minimax Loss
equations.latex: \min_{\omega} \max_{g} \frac{1}{N} \sum_{j=1}^{N} \left\| E\left[\left(1 - \sum_{i=1}^{N} \omega(I_t, I_{t,i}) R^e_{t+1,i}\right) R^e_{t+1,j} g(I_t, I_{t,j})\right] \right\|^2

equations.name: Empirical Loss Function
equations.latex: L(\omega | g, I_t, I_{t,i}) = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N} \frac{T_i}{T} \left\| \frac{1}{T_i} \sum_{t \in T_i} M_{t+1} R^e_{t+1,i} \hat{g}(I_t, I_{t,i}) \right\|^2
equations.notes: Eq. (3). Weighted by T_i/T for unbalanced panel.

equations.name: LSTM Cell Update
equations.latex: \tilde{c}_t = \tanh(W_h^{(c)} h_{t-1} + W_x^{(c)} x_t + w_0^{(c)}), \quad c_t = f_t \circ c_{t-1} + i_t \circ \tilde{c}_t, \quad h_t = o_t \circ \tanh(c_t)

equations.name: Optimal SDF Weights
equations.notes: Eq. (1) — the GAN implicitly finds this without inverting a large covariance matrix.

tensors.name: macro_series
tensors.description: 178 macroeconomic time series at each month t

tensors.name: firm_chars
tensors.description: 46 firm-specific characteristics for N stocks at time t, cross-sectionally rank-normalized to quantiles

tensors.name: excess_returns
tensors.description: Monthly excess returns R^e_{t+1,i} for N stocks

tensors.name: h_t_macro
tensors.description: LSTM hidden state — 4 macroeconomic state variables for SDF network

tensors.name: h_t_cond
tensors.description: LSTM hidden state — 32 states for conditional network

tensors.name: sdf_input
tensors.description: Concatenation of [h_t (4), firm_chars (46)] for each stock-time pair

tensors.name: omega
tensors.description: SDF portfolio weights per stock per time

tensors.name: M_t
tensors.description: Stochastic discount factor M_{t+1} = 1 - omega_t^T R^e_{t+1}

tensors.name: F_t
tensors.description: SDF factor F_{t+1} = omega_t^T R^e_{t+1} (tangency portfolio return)

tensors.name: beta
tensors.description: Risk loadings beta_{t,i} proportional to E_t[R^e_{t+1,i} F_{t+1}]

ensemble.note: 9 models with different random initializations, averaged

training_procedure.step1: Initialize SDF network; minimize unconditional loss (g=constant)
training_procedure.step2: Fix SDF network; maximize loss over conditional network parameters
training_procedure.step3: Fix conditional network; minimize conditional loss over SDF network
training_procedure.note: Algorithm converges after one pass of these 3 steps (no iterative GAN loop needed)

input_normalization.firm_characteristics: Cross-sectional rank → quantile (0 to 1), centered around mean
input_normalization.macro_variables: Standard transformations per McCracken & Ng (2016): levels, differences, log-differences

batch_structure.note: Full cross-section per time step (all N stocks at time t). Not mini-batch over stocks.

missing_details: Number of training epochs / total gradient steps not explicitly stated
Exact batch size for gradient computation not stated

datasets.name: CRSP U.S. Equities
datasets.description: All U.S. stocks monthly returns 1967-2016, ~10,000 stocks per month with all 46 characteristics available

metrics.name: Sharpe Ratio (SR)
metrics.description: Annual unconditional SR of SDF factor portfolio

metrics.name: Explained Variation (EV)
metrics.description: Time-series R2 of individual stock returns explained by model

metrics.name: Cross-Sectional R2 (XS-R2)
metrics.description: Cross-sectional mean R2 of pricing errors

reported_results.note: Monthly Sharpe Ratios; annual SR_test for GAN = 2.6 (annualized)

implementation_assumptions.assumption: LSTM hidden size equals the number of output state processes (4 for SDF, 32 for conditional)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard LSTM usage; paper only reports number of hidden states output
Domain convention for tabular/finance research; no framework stated in paper
Paper does not mention it; LSTM with gates reduces vanishing/exploding gradient issues

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Beta estimation network shares same architecture as SDF network (2 hidden layers, 64 units)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper describes beta as predicted by a FFN, same input structure as omega network
alternatives: separate hyperparameter search for beta network

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Cross-sectional quantile normalization: rank/(N+1) within each month
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard practice; paper says characteristics converted to quantiles cross-sectionally

implementation_assumptions.assumption: SDF weights normalized: sum(|omega|) = 1 (unit L1 norm mentioned in turnover calculation)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper mentions kwtk_1 = 1 in turnover calculation footnote

ambiguities.description: LSTM internal hidden size not specified — only output hidden state dimension given
Normalization constraint on g(.) function not precisely defined
Exact number of training epochs/iterations not specified
